Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : ❗️ ❗️ ❗️ According to Axios, citing Pentagon officials, the weapons systems officer of a U.S. Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over southern Iran on Friday was injured during ejection but remained mobile. He managed to evade capture in the mountains for more than a day before being located and evacuated by a combat search and rescue (CSAR) team on Sunday morning. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

Open Source Intel Location of U.S. forward base used in the Iran F‑15 rescue mission was near Isfahan, deep inside Iranian territory, close to key military, missile and nuclear infrastructure. @Osinttechnical https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2040711518103163327/photo/1 tweet

OSINTtechnical Location of the USAF forward base set up deep within Iran for the F-15 crew rescue mission. The base was set up just outside of Isfahan, a critical Iranian strategic hub with missile and army bases, nuclear facilities, and the airbase home to Iran’s F-14 fleet. https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2040710710087287056/photo/1 tweet

Visioner ‼️ 🇺🇲 🇮🇷 Apparently, along with 2 MC-130 COMMANDO II special aircraft, U.S. forces also destroyed at least one of their own helicopters, an MH-6 Little Bird, which was damaged before evacuation from a base in Iran and was burned on site. 📸 In the photo, at the bottom left, you can see the main rotor mechanism of the MH-6 LITTLE BIRD helicopter with remnants of its blades. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

Open Source Intel A senior Hamas delegation met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan in Istanbul, including Muhammad Darwish, Khaled Mashal, Khalil al-Hiya, and Zahar Jabarin. The delegation thanked Erdogan for his support of the Palestinian cause, discussing the ceasefire, humanitarian aid to Gaza, and tensions in Jerusalem and the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound. Erdogan reaffirmed Turkey’s strong backing of Palestinian rights and their cause. tweet

BIG: After evacuating the second crew member of the downed F-15E, the U.S. had to destroy two HC-130J Combat King II rescue aircraft and two helicopters that, for unknown reasons, were unable to leave Iran. The aircraft were blown up on the ground to prevent them from falling into Iranian hands after landing at an improvised refueling site. Three additional HC-130J Combat King II aircraft were then dispatched to continue the combat search-and-rescue mission.

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 All-Seeing Chinese 📝 Americans concerned about growth of China's intelligence capabilities Literally from the very beginning of hostilities in Iran , and even more precisely from the moment of preparation for them, satellite images of American bases and ships in the region have been flying across the internet, accompanied by tallies of US assets. The authorship of such publications has always been the same — they were posted by Chinese company MizarVision. Moreover, to conduct detailed analysis of American presence, the Chinese use various AI tools and algorithms , combining them with satellite observation. Therefore, now in the US the narrative is growing louder that the Chinese may be helping Iranians with intelligence — and at the same time anxiety is rising over the sale of such data. 📌 Direct support from China has not yet been confirmed. They continue to maintain a line of formal neutrality, speak about ceasefire and try not to appear as a party to the conflict. Therefore, the available data rather point to a demonstration of technological capabilities than to proven participation in targeting for Tehran. 🖍 But this story should not be underestimated either. Even if today MizarVision and similar firms mainly work as a showcase of capabilities, tomorrow their products could become a convenient tool for indirect support — while maintaining the customary distance that official Chinese authorities have always kept. ❗️ This is the main point of what is happening. China is not rushing to enter the war directly, but is showing that it already knows how to carefully monitor the US military machine. At the same time, local firms are honing their AI tools and, likely, are not forgetting to sell their services, positioning themselves as the "all-seeing eye" of American military activity. #Iran #China #USA 🏮 @rybar_pacific — your ticket to Pacific confusion 💸 Support us Original msg

🇷🇺 Vladimir is one of the oldest cities in Russia, founded in the late 10th century and rising to prominence in the 12th under Prince Andrei Bogolyubsky, who made it the political center of northeastern Rus’. Before Moscow emerged, Vladimir was effectively the capital of the region. Its influence declined in the 14th century as power shifted to Moscow, but Vladimir remained an important religious and administrative center, preserving architecture and traditions that predate the rise of the centralized Russian state. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
